root_text.an6.41:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā sāriputto rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate.
root_text.an6.41:1.3: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sambahulehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ gijjhakūṭā pabbatā orohanto addasa aññatarasmiṁ padese mahantaṁ dārukkhandhaṁ.
root_text.an6.41:1.5: “passatha no, āvuso, tumhe amuṁ mahantaṁ dārukkhandhan”ti?
root_text.an6.41:2.1: “Ākaṅkhamāno, āvuso, bhikkhu iddhimā cetovasippatto amuṁ dārukkhandhaṁ pathavītveva adhimucceyya.
root_text.an6.41:2.3: Atthi, āvuso, amumhi dārukkhandhe pathavīdhātu, yaṁ nissāya bhikkhu iddhimā cetovasippatto amuṁ dārukkhandhaṁ pathavītveva adhimucceyya.
root_text.an6.41:2.4: Ākaṅkhamāno, āvuso, bhikkhu iddhimā cetovasippatto amuṁ dārukkhandhaṁ āpotveva adhimucceyya …pe…
root_text.an6.41:2.10: Atthi, āvuso, amumhi dārukkhandhe asubhadhātu, yaṁ nissāya bhikkhu iddhimā cetovasippatto amuṁ dārukkhandhaṁ asubhantveva adhimucceyyā”ti.

translation_text.an6.41:1.2: At one time Venerable Sāriputta was staying near Rājagaha, on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain.
translation_text.an6.41:1.3: Then Venerable Sāriputta robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, descended the Vulture’s Peak together with several mendicants. At a certain spot he saw a large tree trunk,
translation_text.an6.41:1.5: “Reverends, do you see this large tree trunk?”
translation_text.an6.41:2.1: “If they wanted to, a mendicant with psychic powers who has mastered their mind could determine this tree trunk to be nothing but earth.
translation_text.an6.41:2.3: Because the earth element exists in the tree trunk. Relying on that a mendicant with psychic powers could determine it to be nothing but earth.
translation_text.an6.41:2.4: If they wanted to, a mendicant with psychic powers who has mastered their mind could determine this tree trunk to be nothing but water. …
translation_text.an6.41:2.5: Or they could determine it to be nothing but fire …
translation_text.an6.41:2.6: Or they could determine it to be nothing but air …
translation_text.an6.41:2.7: Or they could determine it to be nothing but beautiful …
translation_text.an6.41:2.8: Or they could determine it to be nothing but ugly.
translation_text.an6.41:2.10: Because the element of ugliness exists in the tree trunk. Relying on that a mendicant with psychic powers could determine it to be nothing but ugly.”
